steps.content: Identify the starting value: at sea level ($x = 0$ feet), the boiling point is $198^{\circ}$F. This is the constant term in the equation.

Determine the rate of change: for every 520 feet of altitude, the boiling point decreases by $1^{\circ}$F. The rate is $-\dfrac{1}{520}$ degrees per foot, so after $x$ feet the total decrease is $\dfrac{x}{520}$ degrees.

Evaluate all choices:
  • $B = 520 + \frac{x}{198}$: $B = 520 + \dfrac{x}{198}$ - the constants are swapped and the sign is wrong; boiling point would increase with altitude. Eliminated.
  • $B = 520 - \frac{x}{198}$: $B = 520 - \dfrac{x}{198}$ - constants are swapped entirely; starts at 520 instead of 198. Eliminated.
  • $B = 198 + \frac{x}{520}$: $B = 198 + \dfrac{x}{520}$ - starts correctly at 198 but adds instead of subtracts, so boiling point would rise with altitude. Eliminated.
  • $B = 198 - \frac{x}{520}$: $B = 198 - \dfrac{x}{520}$ - starts at 198 and decreases by $\dfrac{x}{520}$ for every $x$ feet above sea level. That matches the correct answer - D is correct.
